_instructions: This is the full Mockingbird JSON format. Use it when you need:
- Dynamic responses (echo request values back in the response body)
Fields starting with _ are optional metadata/comments.
Remove this _instructions block before uploading (or leave it — Mockingbird ignores _ fields).

stub.name: FILL IN: Your Stub Name
stub.description: FILL IN: What this stub simulates

request._comment: Define what requests this stub will intercept.
request._url_note: Use {paramName} for path variables. Use url-pattern for regex matching.

headers._optional_note: Add optional headers below if needed

scenarios._note: Put specific cases BEFORE the default. First match wins.

_type_options: body-contains      — Request body (text) contains a string
header-equals      — Header equals value (use: HeaderName == value)
always             — Always matches (use only for the last/default scenario)
match.value: FILL IN: e.g., /99999 or the string to match

delay._comment: Delete 'delay' block if no delay needed

scenarios._scenario: SCENARIO 2 — Server error for resilience testing

match.value: FILL IN: e.g., /error or specific ID

scenarios._scenario: SCENARIO 3 — Dynamic response (echoes request values back)
scenarios._note: Use {{placeholders}} to extract values from the request and include in the response

_placeholder_reference.{{request.pathParam.NAME}}: Named path variable from URL — e.g., {customerId} in /customers/{customerId}
_placeholder_reference.{{now}}: Current UTC timestamp in ISO 8601 format
_placeholder_reference.{{randomInt lower=1 upper=1000}}: Random integer between lower and upper

match.value: FILL IN: regex to match — e.g., /api/v1/customers/[0-9]+

body._note: Replace all placeholder values with real data or use {{placeholders}} for dynamic values
body.name: FILL IN: John Smith

scenarios._scenario: DEFAULT — catches everything else (MUST be last)

body.FILL IN: Replace this with your default success response
